pros: There are a lot of people, but it’s far from being over crowded.
The city center is small enough that you can walk almost everywhere, but large enough that you can still get tired doing it.
Even though the area around the city has a lot of elevation, the city itself is flat.
Public transportation is decent; you can get around easily with the buses.
It has a lot of nice parks.
tips: Jardí del Túria is a great place for outdoor sports. It has separate lanes for walking, running, and biking, plus sports fields and simple outdoor gyms.
personalNotes: There are a lot of Dutch people here.
It felt like a very nice place to settle down and live.

haveBeen.name: Kukla
comments: It can be quite busy and it’s a small place, so reserving beforehand is a good idea.

haveBeen.name: Gino Banana
comments: You can order very small pizzas to share.
The owner's first restaurant with the same concept and food is La Finestra.

haveBeen.name: The Wine Gallery
comments: It has a very large wine selection, including many local wines.

haveBeen.name: Café ArtySana - Ruzafa
comments: Large range of food and drinks.

haveBeen.name: Amor Amargo
comments: On the fancier side, but not too expensive.

haveBeen.name: St. Patrick's
comments: Most people can usually speak English.

wantToGo.name: La Finestra
comments: You can order very small pizzas to share.
Gino Banana has a very similar concept and food and is less busy.

wantToGo.name: Cervecería Baden Baden
comments: It has a great selection of beers.

goingOut.name: La Diva
notes: Entry at the door usually includes a drink

goingOut.name: Marina
notes: Usually better to buy tickets in advance

goingOut.name: Mya / Umbracle
notes: Expensive, but a very cool location

goingOut.name: La Fabrica de Hielo
notes: Also nice for drinks during the day and in the evening

barsForDrinks.name: Cafe Negrito
notes: The rest of Plaza Negrito is also great
